   6.2 Maralinga Downs, Warribrook, Markov and Retreat. (P20/2153, P20/2007).
Historical exploration has shown that old workings in the Warribrook – Maralinga Downs - Hidden Treasure area are proximal to NE to NNE
aeromagnetic structures. Drilling around old gold workings that straddle these aeromagnetic structures to the south by Dingo Ridge Metals in
2002 intersected moderate amounts of gold, shown in Figure 5.  Significant intersections include 3m @ 1.4 g/t Au at Gem, 4m @ 9.05 g/t
Au and 4m @ 2.98 g/t Au at Hidden Treasure Extended, and 4m @ 2.28 g/t Au at Hidden Treasure South.
 
Along these structures to the north, drilling by Mt Callan Mining and Redgum Bell Operations from 1994 to 1996 also intersected anomalous gold
assays. Aircore holes, drilled by Mt Callan Mining (Davie_Smythe, 1994) only tested the soil anomalies along the Hidden Treasure Line of
workings. Intersections include 3m @ 2.5 g/t Au, 2m @ 1.3 g/t Au and 2m @ 8.8 g/t Au, illustrated in hole MBAC236 in Figure 5. The 20
ppb Au soil contour north of Warribrook, within E20/TENEMENT_A has not been drill tested.
